results.query: trying to make our household products more ethical without breaking the bank

responses.claude: Start by switching one product at a time to ethical alternatives—focus on items you use most frequently or that have the biggest environmental impact, like cleaning supplies or personal care products. Look for budget-friendly options at discount retailers, buy generic eco-friendly brands, or make your own cleaners using basic ingredients like vinegar, baking soda, and essential oils. You can also extend product life and reduce waste by using concentrated versions, refillable options, or buying in bulk, which often saves money while being more sustainable.
